what: R Bindings to FastHPOCR Phenotype Concept Recognition
title: R Bindings to FastHPOCR Phenotype Concept Recognition
text: RfastHPOCR reticulate-backed R binding Tudor Groza’s FastHPOCR Python package fast concept recognition Human Phenotype Ontology terms free text. package keeps upstream Python implementation execution engine adds R-oriented helpers : declaring installing Python dependency set (FastHPOCR, pronto, tqdm), downloading ontology source files, building HPO, MONDO, ORPHANET, SNOMED indexes, loading FastHPOCR annotator index file, converting annotation objects R data frames explicit offset columns.

title: R Bindings to FastHPOCR Phenotype Concept Recognition
text: Install GitHub remotes::install_github("sounkou-bioinfo/RfastHPOCR"). ordinary use, RfastHPOCR follows current reticulate package practice: library(RfastHPOCR) declares Python requirements reticulate::py_require(), reticulate can resolve ephemeral virtual environment Python first initialized. FastHPOCR 0.1.4 PyPI currently declare runtime dependencies, RfastHPOCR requests pronto tqdm explicitly. prefer persistent named environment, create select Python initialized: persistent environment, run fast_hpo_cr_install(envname = "r-fast-hpo-cr", method = "virtualenv"), restart R, call fast_hpo_cr_use_env("r-fast-hpo-cr", method = "virtualenv") first Python import. install upstream GitHub repository instead PyPI: install Python dependency upstream GitHub repository instead PyPI, use fast_hpo_cr_install(source = "github", ref = "main"). Python already initialized selecting installing environment, restart R reticulate can bind intended environment.
code: library(RfastHPOCR) fast_hpo_cr_python_packages() #> [1] "FastHPOCR>=0.1.4" "pronto"           "tqdm" fast_hpo_cr_available() #> [1] TRUE

title: R Bindings to FastHPOCR Phenotype Concept Recognition
text: HPO primary target. HPO release assets published https://github.com/obophenotype/human-phenotype-ontology/releases. hp.obo helper uses stable OBO PURL source file used FastHPOCR’s HPO indexer. Additional HPO JSON, OWL, HPOA, gene/phenotype tables exposed validation audit workflows. real HPO analysis, use download_hpo_obo("ontologies") fetch indexing source, plus optional audit resources download_hpo_json(), download_hpo_phenotype_hpoa(), download_hpo_genes_to_phenotype(). MONDO Orphanet download helpers present upstream FastHPOCR indexers, focus package. MONDO release assets published https://github.com/monarch-initiative/mondo/releases. rest README mock API sketch: creates tiny HPO-style OBO file, runs HPO download helper local file source, indexes multiple ways Python FastHPOCR engine, annotates text, writes annotation table. local file source keeps README rendering fast network-independent still exercising real download, indexing, re-indexing, annotation, serialization code paths.
code: fast_hpo_cr_ontology_urls()[grep("^hpo_", names(fast_hpo_cr_ontology_urls()))] #>                                                             hpo_obo  #>                            "https://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/hp.obo"  #>                                                            hpo_json  #>                           "https://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/hp.json"  #>                                                             hpo_owl  #>                            "https://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/hp.owl"  #>                                                  hpo_phenotype_hpoa  #>            "https://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/hp/hpoa/phenotype.hpoa"  #>                                              hpo_genes_to_phenotype  #>    "https://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/hp/hpoa/genes_to_phenotype.txt"  #>                                              hpo_phenotype_to_genes  #>    "https://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/hp/hpoa/phenotype_to_genes.txt"  #>                                                hpo_genes_to_disease  #>      "https://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/hp/hpoa/genes_to_disease.txt"  #>                                                       hpo_data_page  #> "https://github.com/obophenotype/human-phenotype-ontology/releases"

title: R Bindings to FastHPOCR Phenotype Concept Recognition
text: Create tiny HPO-like ontology fixture. contains HPO root needed FastHPOCR (HP:0000118), two top-level abnormality categories, two concrete phenotype terms. Index concrete phenotype branches annotate sentence. Re-index ontology restricted nervous-system branch. real second FastHPOCR indexing pass; genitourinary Short stature term present restricted index. Re-index external synonym file. phrase small stature tiny OBO file; comes external synonym file passed FastHPOCR. Serialize R annotation table tab-separated shape used upstream Python package.
code: work_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "RfastHPOCR-readme") unlink(work_dir, recursive = TRUE) dir.create(work_dir, recursive = TRUE)  source_obo <- file.path(work_dir, "source-hp.obo") writeLines(c(   "format-version: 1.2",   "ontology: hp",   "",   "[Term]",   "id: HP:0000001",   "name: All",   "",   "[Term]",   "id: HP:0000118",   "name: Phenotypic abnormality",   "is_a: HP:0000001 ! All",   "",   "[Term]",   "id: HP:0000119",   "name: Abnormality of the genitourinary system",   "is_a: HP:0000118 ! Phenotypic abnormality",   "",   "[Term]",   "id: HP:0000707",   "name: Abnormality of the nervous system",   "is_a: HP:0000118 ! Phenotypic abnormality",   "",   "[Term]",   "id: HP:0004322",   "name: Short stature",   "synonym: \"short height\" EXACT []",   "is_a: HP:0000119 ! Abnormality of the genitourinary system",   "",   "[Term]",   "id: HP:0001250",   "name: Seizure",   "synonym: \"seizures\" EXACT []",   "is_a: HP:0000707 ! Abnormality of the nervous system" ), source_obo)  hp_obo <- download_hpo_obo(   dest_dir = file.path(work_dir, "downloads"),   url = source_obo,   quiet = TRUE ) basename(hp_obo) #> [1] "hp.obo" index_all_log <- capture.output({   index_all <- index_hpo(     hpo_file = hp_obo,     output_dir = file.path(work_dir, "index-all"),     root_concepts = c("HP:0000119", "HP:0000707"),     include_top_level_category = TRUE,     compress_index = FALSE   ) }) grep("Collected|Indexing HPO terms|Serializing index", index_all_log, value = TRUE) #> [1] " - Collected 2 terms."     " - Indexing HPO terms ..." #> [3] " - Serializing index ..." file.exists(index_all) #> [1] TRUE  ann_all <- hpo_annotator(index_all) hits_all <- hpo_annotate(   ann_all,   "The patient has short stature and seizures.",   longest_match = TRUE ) hits_all[, c("span", "id", "label", "start", "end", "start_offset", "end_offset")] #>            span         id         label start end start_offset end_offset #> 1 short stature HP:0004322 Short stature    17  29           16         29 #> 2      seizures HP:0001250      seizures    35  42           34         42 index_neuro_log <- capture.output({   index_neuro <- index_hpo(     hpo_file = hp_obo,     output_dir = file.path(work_dir, "index-neuro"),     root_concepts = "HP:0000707",     include_top_level_category = TRUE,     compress_index = FALSE   ) }) grep("Collected|Indexing HPO terms|Serializing index", index_neuro_log, value = TRUE) #> [1] " - Collected 1 terms."     " - Indexing HPO terms ..." #> [3] " - Serializing index ..."  ann_neuro <- hpo_annotator(index_neuro) hpo_annotate(   ann_neuro,   "The patient has short stature and seizures.",   longest_match = TRUE )[, c("span", "id", "label")] #>       span         id    label #> 1 seizures HP:0001250 seizures syn_file <- file.path(work_dir, "external-synonyms.txt") writeLines("HP:0004322=small stature", syn_file)  index_external_log <- capture.output({   index_external <- index_hpo(     hpo_file = hp_obo,     output_dir = file.path(work_dir, "index-external"),     root_concepts = c("HP:0000119", "HP:0000707"),     external_syn_file = syn_file,     include_top_level_category = TRUE,     compress_index = FALSE   ) }) grep("Collected|Indexing HPO terms|Serializing index", index_external_log, value = TRUE) #> [1] " - Collected 2 terms."     " - Indexing HPO terms ..." #> [3] " - Serializing index ..."  ann_external <- hpo_annotator(index_external) hpo_annotate(ann_external, "Small stature was noted.")[, c("span", "id", "label")] #>            span         id         label #> 1 Small stature HP:0004322 small stature out_tsv <- file.path(work_dir, "annotations.tsv") hpo_write_annotations(hits_all, out_tsv, include_categories = TRUE) readLines(out_tsv) #> [1] "[16:29]\tHP:0004322\tShort stature\tshort stature\t[HP:0000119 (Abnormality of the genitourinary system)]" #> [2] "[34:42]\tHP:0001250\tseizures\tseizures\t[HP:0000707 (Abnormality of the nervous system)]"

what: Role in the hybrid HPO extraction pipeline
title: R Bindings to FastHPOCR Phenotype Concept Recognition
text: intended split : FastHPOCR: deterministic extractor/linker baseline explicit phenotype mentions. Small local model: phrase cleanup, paraphrase normalization, candidate phenotype suggestions. piknit / Pi: auditable orchestration R Markdown Quarto, including local tool calls, model calls, report generation. design package owns deterministic FastHPOCR lane: text , candidate HPO rows , provenance offsets preserved.

title: R Bindings to FastHPOCR Phenotype Concept Recognition
text: package now also provides small, model-agnostic pieces needed compare hybrid configurations without making RfastHPOCR depend one LLM provider. main practical arm : FastHPOCR still generates deterministic candidates. model asked short auditable fields: decision, evidence_span, short_reason. Reasoning-token counts, provider exposes , stored run metadata. harness modes explicit tool_only, model_only, hybrid arms can compared term-level schema. prompt embeds candidate rows strict JSON schema. README prints beginning prompt keep rendered document short. model response expected JSON. mocked response demonstrates clinical audit shape: keep direct patient findings drop negated findings evidence quote short reason. hpo_adjudicate_candidates() accepts model runner function. real pipeline runner can call piknit::pi_run(), Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, remote API. run log captures provider/model metadata, token usage, latency, parse success, reasoning-token counts available.
code: note + FastHPOCR candidates -> model keep/drop adjudication review_text <- "Short stature was noted. No seizures were reported." review_hits <- hpo_annotate(ann_all, review_text, longest_match = TRUE) review_candidates <- hpo_candidate_table(review_hits, case_id = "readme-case-001") review_candidates[, c("case_id", "candidate_id", "candidate_span", "hpo_id", "hpo_label")] #>           case_id         candidate_id candidate_span     hpo_id     hpo_label #> 1 readme-case-001 readme-case-001:0001  Short stature HP:0004322 Short stature #> 2 readme-case-001 readme-case-001:0002       seizures HP:0001250      seizures hpo_harness_modes()[, c("mode", "question")] #>                           mode #> 1                    tool_only #> 2                   model_only #> 3            model_tools_model #> 4 model_candidates_tools_model #> 5             candidates_model #> 6       candidates_tools_model #>                                                                  question #> 1                              What is the deterministic FastHPOCR floor? #> 2              Can the model extract and map HPO terms without grounding? #> 3                       Does ontology grounding fix model mapping errors? #> 4 Does model-generated candidate expansion plus grounding improve recall? #> 5                 Does model cleanup fix FastHPOCR context noise cheaply? #> 6 Does ontology validation after candidate generation add useful hygiene? prompt <- hpo_adjudication_prompt(review_text, review_candidates) cat(paste(head(strsplit(prompt, "\n", fixed = TRUE)[[1]], 16), collapse = "\n")) #> You are adjudicating candidate Human Phenotype Ontology (HPO) extractions from de-identified clinical text. #> Return only valid JSON matching the schema. Do not include markdown fences or explanatory prose outside JSON. #> Do not provide hidden chain-of-thought. The clinical audit fields are decision, evidence_span, and short_reason. #> Reasoning-token counts, if available from the API, are run metadata and are not a clinical audit field. #>  #> Prompt version: rfasthpocr-candidates-model-v1 #> Case id: readme-case-001 #>  #> Decision rules: #> - Return exactly one decision for each supplied candidate_id. #> - decision = 'keep' only if the phenotype is present for the patient in the note. #> - decision = 'drop' if the mention is negated, family-history-only, uncertain/not established, not about the patient, too generic, duplicated by a more specific kept candidate, or unsupported. #> - patient_context must be one of: patient, family_history, negated, uncertain. #> - evidence_span should be the shortest exact quote from the note that supports the decision, including negation or family-history wording when relevant. #> - short_reason should be one concise sentence; do not include chain-of-thought. #> - If the supplied HPO term is wrong but a better HPO term is obvious from the same evidence, keep decision='drop' and fill replacement_hpo_id/replacement_hpo_label. mock_response <- jsonlite::toJSON(   list(     case_id = "readme-case-001",     decisions = list(       list(         candidate_id = "readme-case-001:0001",         candidate_span = "Short stature",         normalized_phrase = "Short stature",         hpo_id = "HP:0004322",         hpo_label = "Short stature",         decision = "keep",         support_type = "direct",         patient_context = "patient",         evidence_span = "Short stature was noted",         short_reason = "The phenotype is directly stated for the patient.",         replacement_hpo_id = NULL,         replacement_hpo_label = NULL,         confidence = 0.95       ),       list(         candidate_id = "readme-case-001:0002",         candidate_span = "seizures",         normalized_phrase = "seizures",         hpo_id = "HP:0001250",         hpo_label = "Seizure",         decision = "drop",         support_type = "none",         patient_context = "negated",         evidence_span = "No seizures were reported",         short_reason = "The note explicitly negates seizures.",         replacement_hpo_id = NULL,         replacement_hpo_label = NULL,         confidence = 0.99       )     )   ),   auto_unbox = TRUE,   null = "null",   pretty = TRUE )  adjudicated <- hpo_parse_adjudication(mock_response, candidates = review_candidates) adjudicated[, c(   "candidate_span", "hpo_id", "decision", "patient_context",   "evidence_span", "short_reason" )] #>   candidate_span     hpo_id decision patient_context             evidence_span #> 1  Short stature HP:0004322     keep         patient   Short stature was noted #> 2       seizures HP:0001250     drop         negated No seizures were reported #>                                        short_reason #> 1 The phenotype is directly stated for the patient. #> 2             The note explicitly negates seizures. mock_runner <- function(prompt) {   out <- mock_response   attr(out, "usage") <- list(     input_tokens = 500L,     output_tokens = 160L,     total_tokens = 660L,     reasoning_tokens = 0L,     tool_call_count = 0L   )   out }  run <- hpo_adjudicate_candidates(   review_text,   review_candidates,   runner = mock_runner,   provider = "mock-provider",   model = "mock-small-model",   run_id = "readme-run-001" ) run$run_log[, c(   "provider", "model", "mode", "input_tokens", "output_tokens",   "reasoning_tokens", "latency_seconds", "parse_success" )] #>        provider            model             mode input_tokens output_tokens #> 1 mock-provider mock-small-model candidates_model          500           160 #>   reasoning_tokens latency_seconds parse_success #> 1                0    1.525879e-05          TRUE

title: R Bindings to FastHPOCR Phenotype Concept Recognition
text: tiny ontology keeps ordinary README fast deterministic. real HPO smoke test, set RFASTHPOCR_README_REAL_HPO=true run make rdm-real-hpo. path downloads real hp.obo, builds real FastHPOCR HPO index RFASTHPOCR_REAL_HPO_DIR user cache directory, runs context-heavy clinical note harness primitives. intentionally opt-full HPO index large can take several minutes build, code real executable README code, pseudo-code. real HPO candidate layer deliberately captures true proband findings context traps: negated terms, family-history terms, duplicate mentions. exactly model layer produce explicit keep/drop decisions evidence quote. optional real-HPO test also shows boundary deterministic candidate layer model-expansion arms: phrases “floppy infancy”, “talking yet”, “learns slowly”, “staring spells” may require model_only model_candidates_tools_model runs want inferred paraphrased phenotype recovery.
code: real_hpo_dir <- Sys.getenv(   "RFASTHPOCR_REAL_HPO_DIR",   file.path(tools::R_user_dir("RfastHPOCR", "cache"), "real-hpo") ) dir.create(real_hpo_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  real_hp_obo <- file.path(real_hpo_dir, "hp.obo") if (!file.exists(real_hp_obo)) {   real_hp_obo <- download_hpo_obo(real_hpo_dir, quiet = TRUE) }  real_index_dir <- file.path(real_hpo_dir, "index") real_index <- file.path(real_index_dir, "hp.index") if (!file.exists(real_index)) {   dir.create(real_index_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)   real_index_log <- capture.output({     real_index <- index_hpo(       real_hp_obo,       real_index_dir,       root_concepts = "HP:0000118",       include_top_level_category = TRUE,       compress_index = FALSE     )   }) } else {   real_index <- normalizePath(real_index, mustWork = TRUE)   real_index_log <- "Using cached real HPO index." }  data.frame(   hp_obo = basename(real_hp_obo),   hpo_index = basename(real_index),   index_size_mb = round(file.info(real_index)$size / 1024^2, 1),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) #>   hp_obo hpo_index index_size_mb #> 1 hp.obo  hp.index         132.7 stress_note <- paste(   "The patient is a 6-year-old boy referred for genetics evaluation. He has global developmental delay, autism spectrum disorder, intellectual disability, brachydactyly, one hypomelanotic macule on the trunk, and one café-au-lait macule on the neck. Echocardiogram showed cardiomyopathy and a septal cardiac defect.",   "He has no seizures, no hypotonia, no ataxia, and no hearing loss. There is no short stature and no microcephaly.",   "Family history is notable for a brother with thrombocytosis and autism and a maternal aunt with cardiomyopathy. These findings are not present in the proband.",   "Earlier notes described the child as floppy in infancy and not talking yet at 3 years of age. Teachers report that he learns slowly. Parents also describe occasional staring spells.",   sep = "\n\n" )  real_ann <- hpo_annotator(real_index) stress_hits <- hpo_annotate(real_ann, stress_note, longest_match = TRUE) stress_candidates <- hpo_candidate_table(   stress_hits,   case_id = "hpo-stress-001",   source = "FastHPOCR-real-HPO" )  stress_candidates[, c(   "candidate_span", "hpo_id", "hpo_label", "start_offset", "end_offset" )] #>                candidate_span     hpo_id                   hpo_label #> 1  global developmental delay HP:0001263 Developmental delay, global #> 2    autism spectrum disorder HP:0000729    Autism spectrum disorder #> 3                      autism HP:0000717                      Autism #> 4     intellectual disability HP:0001249     Intellectual disability #> 5               brachydactyly HP:0001156               Brachydactyly #> 6        hypomelanotic macule HP:0009719       Hypomelanotic macules #> 7         café-au-lait macule HP:0000957        Cafe-au-lait macules #> 8              cardiomyopathy HP:0001638              Cardiomyopathy #> 9              cardiomyopathy HP:0001638              Cardiomyopathy #> 10      septal cardiac defect HP:0001671         Heart septal defect #> 11                   seizures HP:0001250                     Seizure #> 12                  hypotonia HP:0001252                   Hypotonia #> 13                     ataxia HP:0001251                      Ataxia #> 14               hearing loss HP:0000365                Hearing loss #> 15              short stature HP:0004322               Short stature #> 16               microcephaly HP:0000252                Microcephaly #> 17             thrombocytosis HP:0001894              Thrombocytosis #>    start_offset end_offset #> 1            73         99 #> 2           101        125 #> 3           492        498 #> 4           127        150 #> 5           152        165 #> 6           171        191 #> 7           214        233 #> 8           269        283 #> 9           524        538 #> 10          290        311 #> 11          324        332 #> 12          337        346 #> 13          351        357 #> 14          366        378 #> 15          392        405 #> 16          413        425 #> 17          473        487 negated_ids <- c(   "HP:0001250", # seizures   "HP:0001252", # hypotonia   "HP:0001251", # ataxia   "HP:0000365", # hearing loss   "HP:0004322", # short stature   "HP:0000252"  # microcephaly )  negated_evidence <- c(   "HP:0001250" = "no seizures",   "HP:0001252" = "no hypotonia",   "HP:0001251" = "no ataxia",   "HP:0000365" = "no hearing loss",   "HP:0004322" = "no short stature",   "HP:0000252" = "no microcephaly" )  stress_decision <- function(candidate) {   hpo_id <- as.character(candidate$hpo_id)   start <- as.integer(candidate$start_offset)    if (hpo_id %in% negated_ids) {     return(list(       candidate_id = as.character(candidate$candidate_id),       candidate_span = as.character(candidate$candidate_span),       normalized_phrase = as.character(candidate$normalized_phrase),       hpo_id = hpo_id,       hpo_label = as.character(candidate$hpo_label),       decision = "drop",       support_type = "none",       patient_context = "negated",       evidence_span = unname(negated_evidence[[hpo_id]]),       short_reason = "The note explicitly negates this phenotype for the proband.",       replacement_hpo_id = NULL,       replacement_hpo_label = NULL,       confidence = 1     ))   }    family_history <- hpo_id == "HP:0001894" ||     (hpo_id == "HP:0000717" && start > 450L) ||     (hpo_id == "HP:0001638" && start > 450L)    if (family_history) {     evidence <- if (hpo_id == "HP:0001894") {       "brother with thrombocytosis"     } else if (hpo_id == "HP:0000717") {       "brother with thrombocytosis and autism"     } else {       "maternal aunt with cardiomyopathy"     }     return(list(       candidate_id = as.character(candidate$candidate_id),       candidate_span = as.character(candidate$candidate_span),       normalized_phrase = as.character(candidate$normalized_phrase),       hpo_id = hpo_id,       hpo_label = as.character(candidate$hpo_label),       decision = "drop",       support_type = "none",       patient_context = "family_history",       evidence_span = evidence,       short_reason = "The evidence is family history and is stated not to be present in the proband.",       replacement_hpo_id = NULL,       replacement_hpo_label = NULL,       confidence = 1     ))   }    list(     candidate_id = as.character(candidate$candidate_id),     candidate_span = as.character(candidate$candidate_span),     normalized_phrase = as.character(candidate$normalized_phrase),     hpo_id = hpo_id,     hpo_label = as.character(candidate$hpo_label),     decision = "keep",     support_type = "direct",     patient_context = "patient",     evidence_span = as.character(candidate$candidate_span),     short_reason = "The phenotype is directly stated for the proband.",     replacement_hpo_id = NULL,     replacement_hpo_label = NULL,     confidence = 0.95   ) }  stress_response <- jsonlite::toJSON(   list(     case_id = "hpo-stress-001",     decisions = lapply(seq_len(nrow(stress_candidates)), function(i) {       stress_decision(stress_candidates[i, , drop = FALSE])     })   ),   auto_unbox = TRUE,   null = "null" )  stress_runner <- function(prompt) {   out <- stress_response   attr(out, "usage") <- list(     input_tokens = NA_integer_,     output_tokens = NA_integer_,     total_tokens = NA_integer_,     reasoning_tokens = NA_integer_,     tool_call_count = 0L   )   out }  stress_run <- hpo_adjudicate_candidates(   stress_note,   stress_candidates,   runner = stress_runner,   provider = "expected-output",   model = "manual-stress-adjudicator",   run_id = "hpo-stress-readme" )  stress_run$adjudication[, c(   "candidate_span", "hpo_id", "decision", "patient_context",   "evidence_span", "short_reason" )] #>                candidate_span     hpo_id decision patient_context #> 1  global developmental delay HP:0001263     keep         patient #> 2    autism spectrum disorder HP:0000729     keep         patient #> 3                      autism HP:0000717     drop  family_history #> 4     intellectual disability HP:0001249     keep         patient #> 5               brachydactyly HP:0001156     keep         patient #> 6        hypomelanotic macule HP:0009719     keep         patient #> 7         café-au-lait macule HP:0000957     keep         patient #> 8              cardiomyopathy HP:0001638     keep         patient #> 9              cardiomyopathy HP:0001638     drop  family_history #> 10      septal cardiac defect HP:0001671     keep         patient #> 11                   seizures HP:0001250     drop         negated #> 12                  hypotonia HP:0001252     drop         negated #> 13                     ataxia HP:0001251     drop         negated #> 14               hearing loss HP:0000365     drop         negated #> 15              short stature HP:0004322     drop         negated #> 16               microcephaly HP:0000252     drop         negated #> 17             thrombocytosis HP:0001894     drop  family_history #>                             evidence_span #> 1              global developmental delay #> 2                autism spectrum disorder #> 3  brother with thrombocytosis and autism #> 4                 intellectual disability #> 5                           brachydactyly #> 6                    hypomelanotic macule #> 7                     café-au-lait macule #> 8                          cardiomyopathy #> 9       maternal aunt with cardiomyopathy #> 10                  septal cardiac defect #> 11                            no seizures #> 12                           no hypotonia #> 13                              no ataxia #> 14                        no hearing loss #> 15                       no short stature #> 16                        no microcephaly #> 17            brother with thrombocytosis #>                                                                      short_reason #> 1                               The phenotype is directly stated for the proband. #> 2                               The phenotype is directly stated for the proband. #> 3  The evidence is family history and is stated not to be present in the proband. #> 4                               The phenotype is directly stated for the proband. #> 5                               The phenotype is directly stated for the proband. #> 6                               The phenotype is directly stated for the proband. #> 7                               The phenotype is directly stated for the proband. #> 8                               The phenotype is directly stated for the proband. #> 9  The evidence is family history and is stated not to be present in the proband. #> 10                              The phenotype is directly stated for the proband. #> 11                    The note explicitly negates this phenotype for the proband. #> 12                    The note explicitly negates this phenotype for the proband. #> 13                    The note explicitly negates this phenotype for the proband. #> 14                    The note explicitly negates this phenotype for the proband. #> 15                    The note explicitly negates this phenotype for the proband. #> 16                    The note explicitly negates this phenotype for the proband. #> 17 The evidence is family history and is stated not to be present in the proband.

title: R Bindings to FastHPOCR Phenotype Concept Recognition
text: package follows lightweight R package workflow surrounding packages workspace:
code: make rd             # roxygen2 docs + NAMESPACE make test           # tinytest make index-real-hpo # download/build a cached full HPO index make rdm            # render README.md from README.Rmd make rdm-real-hpo   # render README.md with the full real-HPO stress test make site           # pkgdown site make check          # R CMD check --as-cran --no-manual

title: R bindings to FastHPOCR — RfastHPOCR-package
text: RfastHPOCR provides small R interface upstream Python FastHPOCR package using reticulate. exposes helpers installing Python dependency set, building FastHPOCR ontology indexes, creating annotators, converting annotation objects R data frames.

title: R bindings to FastHPOCR — RfastHPOCR-package
text: Maintainer: Sounkou Mahamane Toure sounkoutoure@gmail.com contributors: Tudor Groza (Author copyright holder upstream 'FastHPOCR' Python package) [contributor, copyright holder]

what: Download ontology source files for FastHPOCR indexing — download_hpo_obo
title: Download ontology source files for FastHPOCR indexing — download_hpo_obo
text: helpers download commonly used ontology source files local directory return resulting path. download_hpo_obo() downloads hp.obo, download_mondo_obo() downloads mondo.obo, download_orphanet_product1_xml() downloads Orphanet en_product1.xml.

title: Download ontology source files for FastHPOCR indexing — download_hpo_obo
code: download_hpo_obo(   dest_dir = ".",   filename = "hp.obo",   overwrite = FALSE,   url = HPO_OBO_URL,   quiet = FALSE )  download_mondo_obo(   dest_dir = ".",   filename = "mondo.obo",   overwrite = FALSE,   url = MONDO_OBO_URL,   quiet = FALSE )  download_orphanet_product1_xml(   dest_dir = ".",   filename = "en_product1.xml",   overwrite = FALSE,   url = ORPHANET_PRODUCT1_XML_URL,   quiet = FALSE )  download_orphanet_product1_json(   dest_dir = ".",   xml_file = NULL,   json_filename = "en_product1.json",   overwrite = FALSE,   url = ORPHANET_PRODUCT1_XML_URL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Download ontology source files for FastHPOCR indexing — download_hpo_obo
text: dest_dir Directory file written. filename Output filename within dest_dir. overwrite Replace existing file. url Source URL. mainly exposed mirrors tests. quiet Passed utils::download.file(). xml_file Existing Orphanet XML file. NULL, XML downloaded first download_orphanet_product1_xml(). json_filename Output JSON filename within dest_dir.

title: Download ontology source files for FastHPOCR indexing — download_hpo_obo
text: normalized path downloaded file.

title: Download ontology source files for FastHPOCR indexing — download_hpo_obo
text: FastHPOCR's ORPHANET indexer expects small JSON shape rather XML served Orphadata, download_orphanet_product1_json() downloads XML converts fields used FastHPOCR.

title: Download ontology source files for FastHPOCR indexing — download_hpo_obo
code: if (FALSE) {   hp <- download_hpo_obo("ontologies")   mondo <- download_mondo_obo("ontologies") } if (FALSE) {   orpha_json <- download_orphanet_product1_json("ontologies") }

what: Download HPO ontology and annotation resources — download_hpo_resource
title: Download HPO ontology and annotation resources — download_hpo_resource
text: Downloads HPO resources documented HPO ontology data page. Use resource = "obo" FastHPOCR indexing; JSON, OWL, HPOA/text resources included downstream validation, annotation audit, gene/phenotype lookups.

title: Download HPO ontology and annotation resources — download_hpo_resource
code: download_hpo_resource(   resource = c("obo", "json", "owl", "phenotype_hpoa", "genes_to_phenotype",     "phenotype_to_genes", "genes_to_disease"),   dest_dir = ".",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  download_hpo_json(dest_dir = ".", overwrite = FALSE, quiet = FALSE)  download_hpo_owl(dest_dir = ".", overwrite = FALSE, quiet = FALSE)  download_hpo_phenotype_hpoa(dest_dir = ".", overwrite = FALSE, quiet = FALSE)  download_hpo_genes_to_phenotype(   dest_dir = ".",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  download_hpo_phenotype_to_genes(   dest_dir = ".",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  download_hpo_genes_to_disease(dest_dir = ".", overwrite = FALSE, quiet = FALSE)

title: Download HPO ontology and annotation resources — download_hpo_resource
text: resource HPO resource download. dest_dir Directory file written. overwrite Replace existing file. quiet Passed utils::download.file().

text: normalized path downloaded file.

what: Install the Python FastHPOCR dependency set — fast_hpo_cr_install
title: Install the Python FastHPOCR dependency set — fast_hpo_cr_install
text: Installs FastHPOCR plus undeclared Python runtime dependencies named reticulate environment. helper users prefer persistent managed environment; ordinary package use relies reticulate::py_require() .onLoad() can use reticulate's ephemeral virtual environment.

title: Install the Python FastHPOCR dependency set — fast_hpo_cr_install
code: fast_hpo_cr_install(   envname = "r-fast-hpo-cr",   method = c("auto", "virtualenv", "conda"),   source = c("pypi", "github"),   min_version = FAST_HPO_CR_MIN_VERSION,   ref = "main",   ... )

title: Install the Python FastHPOCR dependency set — fast_hpo_cr_install
text: envname Reticulate environment name passed reticulate::py_install(). method Environment backend passed reticulate::py_install(). source Install released PyPI package upstream GitHub source. min_version Minimum PyPI version source = "pypi". Use NULL unpinned. ref Git ref source = "github". ... Additional arguments passed reticulate::py_install().

title: Install the Python FastHPOCR dependency set — fast_hpo_cr_install
text: Invisibly, Python package specifications requested.

title: Install the Python FastHPOCR dependency set — fast_hpo_cr_install
text: Set source = "github" install upstream GitHub repository subdirectory contains setup.py. Python already initialized current R session, restart R installing loading annotator reticulate can bind intended environment. select named environment explicitly, call fast_hpo_cr_use_env() Python initialized.

title: Install the Python FastHPOCR dependency set — fast_hpo_cr_install
code: if (FALSE) {   fast_hpo_cr_install()   fast_hpo_cr_install(source = "github") }

what: Select a named reticulate environment for FastHPOCR — fast_hpo_cr_use_env
title: Select a named reticulate environment for FastHPOCR — fast_hpo_cr_use_env
text: Use want persistent Python environment instead ephemeral environment created py_require() declarations. must called Python initialized current R session.

title: Select a named reticulate environment for FastHPOCR — fast_hpo_cr_use_env
code: fast_hpo_cr_use_env(   envname = "r-fast-hpo-cr",   method = c("virtualenv", "conda"),   required = TRUE )

title: Select a named reticulate environment for FastHPOCR — fast_hpo_cr_use_env
text: envname Environment name. method Environment backend select. required Passed reticulate::use_virtualenv() reticulate::use_condaenv().

text: Invisibly, envname.

title: Select a named reticulate environment for FastHPOCR — fast_hpo_cr_use_env
code: if (FALSE) {   fast_hpo_cr_install(method = "virtualenv")   fast_hpo_cr_use_env(method = "virtualenv") }

what: Run a candidates-to-model adjudication step — hpo_adjudicate_candidates
title: Run a candidates-to-model adjudication step — hpo_adjudicate_candidates
text: Executes generic model runner prompt produced hpo_adjudication_prompt(), parses structured JSON response, returns run-level metadata suitable provider/model comparisons. function deliberately runner-agnostic: pass function calls piknit::pi_run(), local OpenAI-compatible endpoint, Ollama, vLLM, provider.

title: Run a candidates-to-model adjudication step — hpo_adjudicate_candidates
code: hpo_adjudicate_candidates(   note,   candidates,   runner,   case_id = NULL,   provider = NA_character_,   model = NA_character_,   mode = "candidates_model",   prompt_version = "rfasthpocr-candidates-model-v1",   tool_config = "none",   run_id = NULL,   retry_count = 0L,   estimated_cost_usd = NA_real_,   error_on_failure = TRUE,   ... )

title: Run a candidates-to-model adjudication step — hpo_adjudicate_candidates
text: note Character scalar containing source clinical text. candidates Candidate table hpo_candidate_table() raw annotations hpo_annotate(). runner Function taking single prompt string returning character response. may also return list text usage fields, character vector usage attribute. case_id Optional case id. provider Provider label run log. model Model label run log. mode Harness mode label. prompt_version Prompt version label. tool_config Tool configuration label list run log. run_id Optional run id. Generated omitted. retry_count Retry count record run log. estimated_cost_usd Optional cost estimate. error_on_failure TRUE, runner parse errors stop execution. FALSE, errors captured returned run_log. ... Additional arguments passed hpo_adjudication_prompt().

title: Run a candidates-to-model adjudication step — hpo_adjudicate_candidates
text: list prompt, raw_response, adjudication, run_log.

title: Run a candidates-to-model adjudication step — hpo_adjudicate_candidates
text: returned run_log treats token counts reasoning-token counts API usage metadata. useful clinical audit fields remain term-level adjudication table: decision, evidence_span, short_reason.

title: Run a candidates-to-model adjudication step — hpo_adjudicate_candidates
code: ann <- data.frame(   span = "seizures",   id = "HP:0001250",   label = "Seizure",   start = 4L,   end = 11L,   start_offset = 3L,   end_offset = 11L,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ann$categories <- I(list(list())) candidates <- hpo_candidate_table(ann, case_id = "case-1") json <- jsonlite::toJSON(   list(     case_id = "case-1",     decisions = list(list(       candidate_id = "case-1:0001",       candidate_span = "seizures",       normalized_phrase = "seizures",       hpo_id = "HP:0001250",       hpo_label = "Seizure",       decision = "drop",       support_type = "none",       patient_context = "negated",       evidence_span = "No seizures",       short_reason = "The note explicitly negates seizures."     ))   ),   auto_unbox = TRUE ) mock_runner <- function(prompt) json hpo_adjudicate_candidates("No seizures were reported.", candidates, mock_runner)$adjudication #>   case_id candidate_id candidate_span normalized_phrase     hpo_id hpo_label #> 1  case-1  case-1:0001       seizures          seizures HP:0001250   Seizure #>   decision support_type patient_context evidence_span #> 1     drop         none         negated   No seizures #>                            short_reason replacement_hpo_id #> 1 The note explicitly negates seizures.               <NA> #>   replacement_hpo_label confidence    source source_rank start end start_offset #> 1                  <NA>         NA FastHPOCR           1     3  11            3 #>   end_offset run_id #> 1         11   <NA>

what: Build a prompt for candidate-to-model HPO adjudication — hpo_adjudication_prompt
title: Build a prompt for candidate-to-model HPO adjudication — hpo_adjudication_prompt
text: Creates prompt practical hybrid arm: note + FastHPOCR candidates -> model keep/drop adjudication. model asked auditable short fields, hidden chain--thought.

title: Build a prompt for candidate-to-model HPO adjudication — hpo_adjudication_prompt
code: hpo_adjudication_prompt(   note,   candidates,   case_id = NULL,   allow_inferred = FALSE,   prompt_version = "rfasthpocr-candidates-model-v1",   extra_instructions = NULL )

title: Build a prompt for candidate-to-model HPO adjudication — hpo_adjudication_prompt
text: note Character scalar containing source clinical text. candidates Candidate table hpo_candidate_table() raw annotations hpo_annotate(). case_id Optional case id. omitted, inferred candidate table possible. allow_inferred TRUE, prompt allows candidate decisions use support_type = "inferred" clearly supported note. still asks model invent new HPO IDs. prompt_version Version label embedded prompt. extra_instructions Optional additional instruction text.

title: Build a prompt for candidate-to-model HPO adjudication — hpo_adjudication_prompt
text: single prompt string.

title: Build a prompt for candidate-to-model HPO adjudication — hpo_adjudication_prompt
code: ann <- data.frame(   span = "seizures",   id = "HP:0001250",   label = "Seizure",   start = 4L,   end = 11L,   start_offset = 3L,   end_offset = 11L,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ann$categories <- I(list(list())) candidates <- hpo_candidate_table(ann, case_id = "case-1") prompt <- hpo_adjudication_prompt("No seizures were reported.", candidates) cat(substr(prompt, 1, 200)) #> You are adjudicating candidate Human Phenotype Ontology (HPO) extractions from de-identified clinical text. #> Return only valid JSON matching the schema. Do not include markdown fences or explanatory pr

what: JSON schema for model adjudication of HPO candidates — hpo_adjudication_schema
title: JSON schema for model adjudication of HPO candidates — hpo_adjudication_schema
text: Returns structured output contract hybrid candidate-adjudication step. Token usage reasoning-token counts belong run-level metadata; clinical auditability comes explicit decision, evidence_span, short_reason fields.

title: JSON schema for model adjudication of HPO candidates — hpo_adjudication_schema
code: hpo_adjudication_schema(as_json = FALSE, pretty = TRUE)

title: JSON schema for model adjudication of HPO candidates — hpo_adjudication_schema
text: as_json Return schema pretty JSON instead R list. pretty Pretty-print JSON as_json = TRUE.

title: JSON schema for model adjudication of HPO candidates — hpo_adjudication_schema
text: R list JSON string containing JSON Schema object.

title: JSON schema for model adjudication of HPO candidates — hpo_adjudication_schema
code: schema <- hpo_adjudication_schema() names(schema) #> [1] "$schema"              "title"                "type"                 #> [4] "additionalProperties" "required"             "properties"

what: Annotate free text with a FastHPOCR index — hpo_annotate
title: Annotate free text with a FastHPOCR index — hpo_annotate
text: Runs upstream HPOAnnotator$annotate() method converts returned Python annotation objects R data frame default. Set output = "python" keep raw Python result advanced reticulate workflows.

title: Annotate free text with a FastHPOCR index — hpo_annotate
code: hpo_annotate(   annotator,   text,   longest_match = FALSE,   output = c("data.frame", "list", "python"),   offsets = c("r", "python") )

title: Annotate free text with a FastHPOCR index — hpo_annotate
text: annotator fast_hpo_cr_annotator returned hpo_annotator(), path index file. text Character scalar annotate. longest_match TRUE, ask FastHPOCR keep longest match among overlapping candidates. output Return type: R data.frame, R list, raw Python object. offsets Coordinate convention user-facing start end columns output "python".

title: Annotate free text with a FastHPOCR index — hpo_annotate
text: data frame default, columns span, id, label, start, end, start_offset, end_offset, list-column categories.

title: Annotate free text with a FastHPOCR index — hpo_annotate
text: FastHPOCR reports offsets Python-style zero-based, end-exclusive spans. default offsets = "r" adds R-style start end columns one-based inclusive. original Python offsets always preserved start_offset end_offset.

title: Annotate free text with a FastHPOCR index — hpo_annotate
code: if (FALSE) {   ann <- hpo_annotator("hp.index.gz")   hpo_annotate(ann, "The patient has short stature and seizures.") }

title: Create a FastHPOCR annotator — hpo_annotator
text: Loads FastHPOCR concept-recognition index returns small R wrapper around underlying Python HPOAnnotator instance. upstream class used HPO, MONDO, ORPHANET, SNOMED indexes.

title: Create a FastHPOCR annotator — hpo_annotator
code: hpo_annotator(index_location)

title: Create a FastHPOCR annotator — hpo_annotator
text: index_location Path FastHPOCR index file, compressed uncompressed.

title: Create a FastHPOCR annotator — hpo_annotator
text: object class fast_hpo_cr_annotator.

title: Create a FastHPOCR annotator — hpo_annotator
code: if (FALSE) {   ann <- hpo_annotator("hp.index.gz") }

what: Convert FastHPOCR annotations to a harness candidate table — hpo_candidate_table
title: Convert FastHPOCR annotations to a harness candidate table — hpo_candidate_table
text: Standardizes annotations returned hpo_annotate() candidate table used hybrid model-adjudication prompts comparison harnesses. HPO identifier label preserved FastHPOCR, candidate_id gives row stable handle model keep/drop decisions.

title: Convert FastHPOCR annotations to a harness candidate table — hpo_candidate_table
code: hpo_candidate_table(   annotations,   case_id = NA_character_,   source = "FastHPOCR",   run_id = NA_character_ )

title: Convert FastHPOCR annotations to a harness candidate table — hpo_candidate_table
text: annotations data frame list returned hpo_annotate(). case_id Character scalar identifying source case. NA allowed ad hoc use, real harness runs pass stable case id. source Source label candidates. run_id Optional run id carry forward joined outputs.

title: Convert FastHPOCR annotations to a harness candidate table — hpo_candidate_table
text: data frame one row per candidate columns including case_id, candidate_id, candidate_span, hpo_id, hpo_label, offset metadata.

title: Convert FastHPOCR annotations to a harness candidate table — hpo_candidate_table
code: ann <- data.frame(   span = "short stature",   id = "HP:0004322",   label = "Short stature",   start = 1L,   end = 13L,   start_offset = 0L,   end_offset = 13L,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ann$categories <- I(list(list())) hpo_candidate_table(ann, case_id = "case-1") #>   case_id run_id candidate_id    source source_rank candidate_span #> 1  case-1   <NA>  case-1:0001 FastHPOCR           1  short stature #>   normalized_phrase     hpo_id     hpo_label start end start_offset end_offset #> 1     short stature HP:0004322 Short stature     0  13            0         13 #>   categories #> 1

what: Extract FastHPOCR candidate tables for one or more cases — hpo_extract_candidates
title: Extract FastHPOCR candidate tables for one or more cases — hpo_extract_candidates
text: Loads reuses FastHPOCR annotator runs hpo_annotate() per input text, returning single comparison-friendly candidate table. package's tool_only harness lane.

title: Extract FastHPOCR candidate tables for one or more cases — hpo_extract_candidates
code: hpo_extract_candidates(   annotator,   text,   case_id = NULL,   longest_match = TRUE,   source = "FastHPOCR",   run_id = NA_character_ )

title: Extract FastHPOCR candidate tables for one or more cases — hpo_extract_candidates
text: annotator fast_hpo_cr_annotator returned hpo_annotator(), path index file. text Character vector case texts. case_id Optional character vector case identifiers. omitted, names(text) used present, otherwise sequential identifiers generated. longest_match Passed hpo_annotate(). source Source label candidate table. run_id Optional run id carry forward candidate rows.

title: Extract FastHPOCR candidate tables for one or more cases — hpo_extract_candidates
text: candidate table returned hpo_candidate_table().

title: Extract FastHPOCR candidate tables for one or more cases — hpo_extract_candidates
code: if (FALSE) {   ann <- hpo_annotator("hp.index.gz")   hpo_extract_candidates(     ann,     c(case1 = "Short stature and seizures were reported."),     longest_match = TRUE   ) }

what: List supported HPO extraction harness modes — hpo_harness_modes
title: List supported HPO extraction harness modes — hpo_harness_modes
text: Returns named ablation modes discussed hybrid FastHPOCR plus model phenotyping experiments. package directly implements deterministic candidate lane generic candidates--model adjudication wrapper; modes can use schema prompt contracts project specific model/tool runners.

title: List supported HPO extraction harness modes — hpo_harness_modes
code: hpo_harness_modes()

title: List supported HPO extraction harness modes — hpo_harness_modes
text: data frame mode, flow, question columns.

title: List supported HPO extraction harness modes — hpo_harness_modes
code: hpo_harness_modes() #>                           mode #> 1                    tool_only #> 2                   model_only #> 3            model_tools_model #> 4 model_candidates_tools_model #> 5             candidates_model #> 6       candidates_tools_model #>                                                                  flow #> 1                                 note -> FastHPOCR -> HPO candidates #> 2                                         note -> model -> HPO output #> 3               note -> model -> ontology tools -> model final output #> 4 note -> model -> candidates -> ontology tools -> model final output #> 5         note + FastHPOCR candidates -> model keep/drop adjudication #> 6 note + FastHPOCR candidates -> ontology tools -> model adjudication #>                                                                  question #> 1                              What is the deterministic FastHPOCR floor? #> 2              Can the model extract and map HPO terms without grounding? #> 3                       Does ontology grounding fix model mapping errors? #> 4 Does model-generated candidate expansion plus grounding improve recall? #> 5                 Does model cleanup fix FastHPOCR context noise cheaply? #> 6 Does ontology validation after candidate generation add useful hygiene?

what: Build a FastHPOCR index configuration list — hpo_index_config
title: Build a FastHPOCR index configuration list — hpo_index_config
text: Creates R list using exact configuration keys expected upstream Python package. helper keeps R API snake_case emitting camelCase names consumed FastHPOCR.

title: Build a FastHPOCR index configuration list — hpo_index_config
code: hpo_index_config(   root_concepts = NULL,   allow_3_letter_acronyms = NULL,   include_top_level_category = NULL,   allow_duplicate_entries = NULL,   compress_index = NULL,   external_syn_file = NULL,   ... )

title: Build a FastHPOCR index configuration list — hpo_index_config
text: root_concepts Optional character vector ontology root concepts index. HPO, look like "HP:0000119"; SNOMED, look like "SCTID:64572001" "64572001". allow_3_letter_acronyms Include potentially ambiguous three-letter acronyms. include_top_level_category Include top-level category metadata upstream indexer supports . allow_duplicate_entries Allow duplicate labels synonyms indexed. compress_index Write gzip-compressed index. external_syn_file Optional path external synonym file. passed using upstream key externalSynFile. ... Additional named configuration entries passed unchanged.

title: Build a FastHPOCR index configuration list — hpo_index_config
text: named list suitable index_config argument indexing functions.

title: Build a FastHPOCR index configuration list — hpo_index_config
code: hpo_index_config(   root_concepts = "HP:0000119",   include_top_level_category = TRUE,   compress_index = TRUE ) #> $rootConcepts #> [1] "HP:0000119" #>  #> $includeTopLevelCategory #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $compressIndex #> [1] TRUE #>

what: Parse model adjudication JSON into a data frame — hpo_parse_adjudication
title: Parse model adjudication JSON into a data frame — hpo_parse_adjudication

title: Parse model adjudication JSON into a data frame — hpo_parse_adjudication
code: hpo_parse_adjudication(x, candidates = NULL)

text: x Character response, piknit/Pi reply character vector, list text, content, reply field. candidates Optional candidate table used join offsets source metadata.

title: Parse model adjudication JSON into a data frame — hpo_parse_adjudication
text: data frame one row per adjudicated candidate.

title: Parse model adjudication JSON into a data frame — hpo_parse_adjudication
code: json <- jsonlite::toJSON(   list(     case_id = "case-1",     decisions = list(list(       candidate_id = "case-1:0001",       candidate_span = "seizures",       normalized_phrase = "seizures",       hpo_id = "HP:0001250",       hpo_label = "Seizure",       decision = "drop",       support_type = "none",       patient_context = "negated",       evidence_span = "No seizures",       short_reason = "The note explicitly negates seizures."     ))   ),   auto_unbox = TRUE ) hpo_parse_adjudication(json) #>   case_id candidate_id candidate_span normalized_phrase     hpo_id hpo_label #> 1  case-1  case-1:0001       seizures          seizures HP:0001250   Seizure #>   decision support_type patient_context evidence_span #> 1     drop         none         negated   No seizures #>                            short_reason replacement_hpo_id #> 1 The note explicitly negates seizures.               <NA> #>   replacement_hpo_label confidence #> 1                  <NA>         NA

what: Write or print FastHPOCR annotations — hpo_write_annotations
title: Write or print FastHPOCR annotations — hpo_write_annotations
text: Serializes R annotation records using tab-separated text format upstream FastHPOCR.HPOAnnotator.serialize(): [start:end] id label span, start end original Python-style offsets (start_offset, end_offset).

title: Write or print FastHPOCR annotations — hpo_write_annotations
code: hpo_write_annotations(annotations, file, include_categories = FALSE)  hpo_print_annotations(annotations, include_categories = FALSE)

title: Write or print FastHPOCR annotations — hpo_write_annotations
text: annotations annotation data frame list . file Output file path. include_categories Include category metadata present.

title: Write or print FastHPOCR annotations — hpo_write_annotations
text: Invisibly, file. Invisibly, annotations.

title: Write or print FastHPOCR annotations — hpo_write_annotations
code: ann <- data.frame(   span = "short stature",   id = "HP:0004322",   label = "Short stature",   start = 1L,   end = 13L,   start_offset = 0L,   end_offset = 13L,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ann$categories <- I(list(list())) out <- tempfile() hpo_write_annotations(ann, out) readLines(out) #> [1] "[0:13]\tHP:0004322\tShort stature\tshort stature"

what: Build a FastHPOCR HPO index — index_hpo
title: Build a FastHPOCR HPO index — index_hpo
text: Wraps FastHPOCR.IndexHPO.IndexHPO creates index file hp.obo ontology file. returned path expected index location.

title: Build a FastHPOCR HPO index — index_hpo
code: index_hpo(   hpo_file,   output_dir,   index_config = list(),   root_concepts = NULL,   allow_3_letter_acronyms = NULL,   include_top_level_category = NULL,   allow_duplicate_entries = NULL,   compress_index = NULL,   external_syn_file = NULL,   create_output_dir = TRUE )

title: Build a FastHPOCR HPO index — index_hpo
text: hpo_file Path hp.obo. output_dir Directory hp.index hp.index.gz written. index_config Optional named list upstream FastHPOCR index options. root_concepts Optional character vector ontology root concepts index. HPO, look like "HP:0000119"; SNOMED, look like "SCTID:64572001" "64572001". allow_3_letter_acronyms Include potentially ambiguous three-letter acronyms. include_top_level_category Include top-level category metadata upstream indexer supports . allow_duplicate_entries Allow duplicate labels synonyms indexed. compress_index Write gzip-compressed index. external_syn_file Optional path external synonym file. passed using upstream key externalSynFile. create_output_dir Create output_dir needed.

title: Build a FastHPOCR HPO index — index_hpo
text: Invisibly, expected output index path.

title: Build a FastHPOCR HPO index — index_hpo
code: if (FALSE) {   index_hpo("hp.obo", "hpo-index", compress_index = TRUE) }

what: Build a FastHPOCR MONDO index — index_mondo

text: Wraps FastHPOCR.IndexMONDO.IndexMONDO creates index file MONDO .obo file.

code: index_mondo(   mondo_file,   output_dir,   index_config = list(),   allow_3_letter_acronyms = NULL,   allow_duplicate_entries = NULL,   compress_index = NULL,   external_syn_file = NULL,   create_output_dir = TRUE )

title: Build a FastHPOCR MONDO index — index_mondo
text: mondo_file Path mondo.obo. output_dir Directory mondo.index mondo.index.gz written. index_config Optional named list upstream FastHPOCR index options. allow_3_letter_acronyms Include potentially ambiguous three-letter acronyms. allow_duplicate_entries Allow duplicate labels synonyms indexed. compress_index Write gzip-compressed index. external_syn_file Optional path external synonym file. passed using upstream key externalSynFile. create_output_dir Create output_dir needed.

title: Build a FastHPOCR MONDO index — index_mondo
text: Invisibly, expected output index path.

title: Build a FastHPOCR MONDO index — index_mondo
code: if (FALSE) {   index_mondo("mondo.obo", "mondo-index", compress_index = TRUE) }

what: Build a FastHPOCR ORPHANET index — index_orphanet
title: Build a FastHPOCR ORPHANET index — index_orphanet
text: Wraps FastHPOCR.IndexORPHANET.IndexORPHANET creates index file unpacked Orphanet JSON data file en_product1.json.

title: Build a FastHPOCR ORPHANET index — index_orphanet
code: index_orphanet(   orpha_data_file,   output_dir,   index_config = list(),   allow_3_letter_acronyms = NULL,   allow_duplicate_entries = NULL,   compress_index = NULL,   external_syn_file = NULL,   create_output_dir = TRUE )

title: Build a FastHPOCR ORPHANET index — index_orphanet
text: orpha_data_file Path unpacked Orphanet JSON data file. output_dir Directory orpha.index orpha.index.gz written. index_config Optional named list upstream FastHPOCR index options. allow_3_letter_acronyms Include potentially ambiguous three-letter acronyms. allow_duplicate_entries Allow duplicate labels synonyms indexed. compress_index Write gzip-compressed index. external_syn_file Optional path external synonym file. passed using upstream key externalSynFile. create_output_dir Create output_dir needed.

title: Build a FastHPOCR ORPHANET index — index_orphanet
text: Invisibly, expected output index path.

title: Build a FastHPOCR ORPHANET index — index_orphanet
code: if (FALSE) {   index_orphanet("en_product1.json", "orpha-index", compress_index = TRUE) }

what: Build a FastHPOCR SNOMED index — index_snomed
title: Build a FastHPOCR SNOMED index — index_snomed
text: Wraps FastHPOCR.IndexSNOMED.IndexSNOMED creates index file SNOMED description relationship tables. Upstream FastHPOCR requires one root_concepts SNOMED indexing.

title: Build a FastHPOCR SNOMED index — index_snomed
code: index_snomed(   description_file,   relations_file,   output_dir,   root_concepts,   index_config = list(),   allow_3_letter_acronyms = NULL,   include_top_level_category = NULL,   allow_duplicate_entries = NULL,   compress_index = NULL,   external_syn_file = NULL,   create_output_dir = TRUE )

title: Build a FastHPOCR SNOMED index — index_snomed
text: description_file Path SNOMED description file, example sct2_Description_Full-en_INT_20230630.txt. relations_file Path SNOMED relationship file, example sct2_Relationship_Full_INT_20230630.txt. output_dir Directory snomed.index snomed.index.gz written. root_concepts Character vector SNOMED root concepts. Required. index_config Optional named list upstream FastHPOCR index options. allow_3_letter_acronyms Include potentially ambiguous three-letter acronyms. include_top_level_category Include top-level category metadata upstream indexer supports . allow_duplicate_entries Allow duplicate labels synonyms indexed. compress_index Write gzip-compressed index. external_syn_file Optional path external synonym file. passed using upstream key externalSynFile. create_output_dir Create output_dir needed.

title: Build a FastHPOCR SNOMED index — index_snomed
text: Invisibly, expected output index path.

title: Build a FastHPOCR SNOMED index — index_snomed
code: if (FALSE) {   index_snomed(     "sct2_Description_Full-en_INT_20230630.txt",     "sct2_Relationship_Full_INT_20230630.txt",     "snomed-index",     root_concepts = "SCTID:64572001"   ) }

what: Convert Orphanet product1 XML to FastHPOCR-compatible JSON — orphanet_product1_xml_to_json
title: Convert Orphanet product1 XML to FastHPOCR-compatible JSON — orphanet_product1_xml_to_json
text: Converts OrphaCode, English disorder Name, SynonymList fields Orphadata's en_product1.xml JSON shape consumed upstream FastHPOCR.IndexORPHANET.

title: Convert Orphanet product1 XML to FastHPOCR-compatible JSON — orphanet_product1_xml_to_json
code: orphanet_product1_xml_to_json(   xml_file,   json_file = sub("\\.xml$", ".json", xml_file),   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Convert Orphanet product1 XML to FastHPOCR-compatible JSON — orphanet_product1_xml_to_json
text: xml_file Path en_product1.xml. json_file Output JSON path. Defaults replacing .xml .json. overwrite Replace existing JSON file.

title: Convert Orphanet product1 XML to FastHPOCR-compatible JSON — orphanet_product1_xml_to_json
text: normalized output JSON path.

title: Convert Orphanet product1 XML to FastHPOCR-compatible JSON — orphanet_product1_xml_to_json
code: xml <- tempfile(fileext = ".xml") writeLines(c(   '<JDBOR><DisorderList><Disorder>',   '<OrphaCode>1</OrphaCode><Name lang="en">Demo</Name>',   '<SynonymList><Synonym lang="en">Demo synonym</Synonym></SynonymList>',   '</Disorder></DisorderList></JDBOR>' ), xml) orphanet_product1_xml_to_json(xml, tempfile(fileext = ".json")) #> [1] "/tmp/Rtmp9Ilfew/file19971e8661ce.json"

title: RfastHPOCR 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial reticulate-backed R bindings upstream FastHPOCR Python package. Added helpers Python dependency installation, ontology indexing, annotation, R-friendly annotation serialization. Added hybrid harness primitives candidate tables, structured adjudication prompts, JSON parsing, provider/model run logs, token/reasoning-token usage metadata. Added opt-real-HPO README stress test index-real-hpo / rdm-real-hpo Make targets context-heavy clinical note harness.
